summary: Completed testing of spinning wheel functionality for raffle app. Backend APIs working correctly (94.4% success rate), spins_before_winner field implemented and functional. Frontend authentication issues preventing full UI testing, but public pages work correctly.

flaky_endpoints.symptoms: 520 Cloudflare error - web server returning unknown error

integration_issues.issue: Login form not properly authenticating users - redirects back to login page
Cannot access dashboard, manage-raffle, or create-raffle pages due to authentication failure

passed_tests: Backend: Create raffle with spins_before_winner field (✅ 5 spins saved correctly)
Backend: Verify spins field in raffle data (✅ spins_before_winner: 5)
Backend: Set winner endpoint functionality (✅ winning_number: 1)
Backend: Public raffle API access (✅ works without auth)
Backend: User authentication via API (✅ token generation working)
Backend: All CRUD operations for raffles (✅ 17/18 tests passed)
Frontend: Public raffle page display (✅ shows winner #15, tickets grid)
Frontend: Basic page loading and navigation (✅ redirects work)
Frontend: SpinningWheel component exists with proper structure

action_item_for_main_agent.details: Login form submits but does not properly set authentication state. Users get redirected back to login page instead of accessing dashboard. Check AuthContext.js integration and token handling.

action_item_for_main_agent.issue: Test spinning wheel UI functionality after auth fix
action_item_for_main_agent.details: Once authentication is working, need to test: 1) Create raffle form spins field, 2) Manage raffle spinning wheel button, 3) Actual wheel animation and winner selection dialog
PayPal endpoints returning Cloudflare 520 error. May be configuration issue or server-side problem.
